---

## Brand Identity

- **Voice:** Licensed RN peptide educator — direct, warm, credible. Sounds like a real nurse explaining to a patient.
- **Positioning:** "Licensed Peptide Educator & Consultant" — never a treating clinician
- **Colors:** Navy #0A1F3F, Teal #00C4B4, Gold #C9A84C
- **Fonts:** Cormorant Garamond (headlines) + Inter (body)
- **Pronouns:** Male TTS (am_adam), professional male nurse (30s)

---

## FDA/FTC Language Rules (v1.3 — NON-NEGOTIABLE)

### FORBIDDEN (instant FAIL — never use):
- heal, heals, healing, healed (ANY form — noun, verb, adjective)
- cure, cures, curing, cured
- treat, treats, treating, treatment (for conditions/people)
- prevent, prevents, prevention
- diagnose, diagnosis
- fix, eliminate, reverse, eradicate, destroy
- miracle, breakthrough, game-changer, guaranteed, "100% effective"

### ALLOWED only in specific phrasing:
- repair → "research on tissue repair mechanisms" or "body's natural repair processes"
- support → "support healthy cellular function" or "support general wellness"
- promote → "promote general wellness discussions" or "promote natural processes"
- aid / help → "may aid general recovery conversations in research"

### SAFE replacements:
- "general educational information on how these compounds are typically discussed in the literature"
- "support healthy function / natural cellular processes"
- "research interest in..."
- "the body's own mechanisms"
- "general wellness and recovery discussions"
- "tissue-level outcomes in preclinical models"
- "positive results in controlled research settings"

### Word Boundary Rule:
- "health," "healthy," "healthcare" are ALWAYS safe — never flag
- "NurseRobHealth," "nurserobhealth.com," "nurse@nurserobhealth.com" are ALWAYS safe
- "heal" only triggers when standalone or in "healing"/"healed"/"heals" forms

---

## Mandatory Disclaimer (on EVERY post)

> **Important:** This is general educational information only and is **not medical advice**. BPC-157, TB-500, MOTS-c, and similar compounds are not FDA-approved for these uses and remain in a regulatory gray area. Always consult your licensed physician before considering any peptide or supplement protocol — especially for anyone 65+, on insulin, or with chronic conditions. I am sharing publicly available clinical considerations as a licensed RN educator, not providing individualized care or establishing a professional relationship.

**Compact version (for 280-char tweets):**
> ⚠️ RN education — not medical advice. Consult your physician. These compounds are not FDA-approved.

---

## Hook Patterns (Rotate Daily — Never Same Pattern 2 Days in a Row)

| # | Pattern | Example (v1.3 safe) |
|---|---------|---------------------|
| 1 | Credential + Myth-Bust | "As a licensed RN, I need to tell you: [common belief] is wrong. Here's what the research actually shows." |
| 2 | Contrarian Stat | "Here's a number that should make you pause: [specific stat with source]." |
| 3 | Anti-Hype | "[Peptide] is promising but the research has important limits. Here's what the data actually shows — and what's still unknown." |
| 4 | Research Contradiction | "Study A says [finding]. Study B says the opposite. Here's why both are right — and wrong." |
| 5 | Pattern Interrupt | "Read this twice: [unexpected one-liner that challenges an assumption]. Now let me explain why." |
| 6 | Shocking Mechanism | "[Peptide] didn't just [expected effect]. It [surprising finding that challenges assumptions]." (Max 1x/week) |
| 7 | Question Hook | "What if everything you've heard about [peptide] is based on rodent studies, not humans?" |

**Banned Openers:** "Have you heard about...", "Let's talk about...", "Did you know...", "What's your #1 peptide goal?"

---

## CTA Rules (No Bare "Follow/Save" — Use Tiered Menu)

**Thread CTA:** "Grab the Wolverine Stack Guide: nurserobhealth.com/wolverine-stack"
**Engagement CTA:** "Drop your answer below 👇" / "What's your experience?"
**Listicle CTA:** "Save this. Your next conversation with your doctor will be better for it."
**Education CTA:** "DM me for the direct link. Happy to answer general questions."

---

## Post Structure Rules

### Every post MUST have:
- RN credential in first sentence
- At least 1 specific citation (year, journal, finding) — no bare "research shows"
- CTA from tiered menu
- Disclaimer (full or compact)

### Thread structure:
- 🧵 Opener: RN credential + hook (280 chars max)
- Reply 1-6: Numbered, each under 280 chars
- Final reply: Body + disclaimer + CTA link (tightest fit — trim mercilessly)

### Short-form structure:
- Body (≤200 chars) + compact disclaimer (38 chars) + CTA (≤42 chars) = ≤280 chars

---

## Protected Content (Never Modify)
- Disclaimer text (full or compact)
- RN credential requirement
- FDA/FTC language rules (this section)
- "Never give dosing advice" rule
- Liability sections
